issue_title: User Profile: Setting E-Mail to required not working
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I have enabled Declarative User Profile as explained here [in the documentation](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#user-profile).

After this, the pre-existing `email` attribute seems to no longer be required, regardless of whether I turn on `Required`.

However, when I declare a new attribute `test` and turn on `Required`, I get asked to provide it when logging in.

### Version

19.0

### Expected behavior

The `email` attribute is required and users have to set this when logging in.

### Actual behavior

The `email` attribute is not required and users do not have to set this (e.g. during login).

### How to Reproduce?

1. Start keycloak with `kc.sh start-dev --features=preview`.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fixed handling of required setting for email in user profile.

Resolves #13923

issue_title: `--optimized` is not supported by `kc.sh export`
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I have a realm export script that essentially runs:
```
# note: kc.sh export briefly starts the server including binding a http port, which fails because 8080 is already in use. So we give it a random port instead.
KC_HTTP_PORT=0 kc.sh export --file  ${_EXPORT_FILE_TMP} --users same_file
```

With KC 19.0.1 this logs:
> Changes detected in configuration. Updating the server image.

and then goes on to rebuild, which I don't want and which wasn't there with KC 18.0.2.
I also don't see how the runtime config property `KC_HTTP_PORT` is relevant for rebuilding?

Adding `--optimized` doesn't work:
> Unknown option: '--optimized'

### Version

19.0.1

### Expected behavior

A way to prevent auto-build for `export`.

### Actual behavior

Seemingly no way to prevent auto-build for `export`.

### How to Reproduce?

`kc.sh export --help` -> no `--optimized` (or similar)

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: 19 - update-email feature - email change does not affect the username when "Email as username" option is checked
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Hello,
I am testing the new update_email feature.
If I have the option "Email as username" active when changing email, it changes the email but not its username.
Is there any other option so that the email change affects the username when the "Email as username" option is checked ?
Thanks.

### Version

19

### Expected behavior

If the login option "Email as username" is active, when I change the email it must also change the username.

### Actual behavior

If the login option "Email as username" is active, when I change the email I have the old email as username.

### How to Reproduce?

1. activate the update-email feature
2. have the login option "email as username" activated
3. then log in with a user 
4. go to "personnal info" 
5. and modify his email 
6. the email has been changed but not the username 

### Anything else?

The new feature [update_email](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/7943)
commit_message:
update-email: email change does not affect the username when "Email as username" option is checked (#15583)

Closes #13988

issue_title: Requests to health endpoint still dispatched to worker threads when `http-relative-path` is set
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

This issue was supposed to be fixed by https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/11507:

>Avoid executing health and metrics endpoint in a worker thread as they run in their own worker thread, and avoid creating a KeycloakSession every time those endpoints are invoked. The current behavior is causing transactions to not disassociate from the calling thread and causing Arjuna to wait for them to timeout. Eventually, the server might block during the shutdown.

However, the previous fix did not take into account when the `http-relative-path` is set so that health/metrics requests are not ignored by the `QuarkusRequestFilter` and  are still dispatched to worker threads.

### Version

19.0.1

### Expected behavior

Do not dispatch health/metrics requests to a worker thread.

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

Run the server as follows:

```
./kc.sh -Dquarkus.transaction-manager.default-transaction-timeout=1s start-dev --health-enabled true --http-relative-path /auth/
```

Then, execute multiple requests to `/auth/health` until you get log entries similar to:

```
2022-08-25 19:42:41,449 WARN  [com.arjuna.ats.arjuna] (executor-thread-1) ARJUNA012077: Abort called on already aborted atomic action 0:ffffc0a8000b:8ca1:6307f782:213                        
2022-08-25 19:42:42,449 WARN  [com.arjuna.ats.arjuna] (Transaction Reaper) ARJUNA012117: TransactionReaper::check processing TX 0:ffffc0a8000b:8ca1:6307f782:291 in state  RUN                
2022-08-25 19:42:42,449 WARN  [com.arjuna.ats.arjuna] (Transaction Reaper Worker 0) ARJUNA012095: Abort of action id 0:ffffc0a8000b:8ca1:6307f782:291 invoked while multiple threads active within it.               
```

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Missing a duplicated parameter check in UserInfo endpoint.
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Related to the reported bug in [JIRA](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-16009), UserInfo endpoint should check duplicated parameters of requests but does not check.

### Version

19.0.0

### Expected behavior

When the below request which contains duplicated scope parameters is comming, Keycloak should respond 400 Bad Request.
```
curl -s -v \
    -H "application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
    -d "access_token=<ACCSESS_TOKEN>" \
    -d "access_token=<ACCSESS_TOKEN>" \
    http://<YOUR_HOST>/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/userinfo
```

### Actual behavior

The duplicated access tokens are not checked, then a client get userinfo response with a status code 200.

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Describe the bug

When switching locale on the error page the user is redirected to `http://localhost:8080/?kc_locale=no/`, and the locale is not changed.

issue_title: Setting responseMode in AuthorizationRequest.Metadata does not take effect
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

**Problem** :  With org.keycloak:keycloak-authz-client it is not possible to set "response_mode" in  AuthorizationRequest

response_mode is not sent to the server, when AuthorizationRequest.Metadata#[setResponseMode](https://www.keycloak.org/docs-api/18.0/javadocs/org/keycloak/representations/idm/authorization/AuthorizationRequest.Metadata.html) is set to "decision" 
​
```
       AuthorizationRequest request = new AuthorizationRequest();
        request.addPermission(resource, scope);
        request.setMetadata(metadata);
        request.getMetadata().setResponseMode("decision");
```

The response_mode "decision" is required to receive overall decision from the server.

 In  org.keycloak.authorization.client.util.[HttpMethodAuthenticator](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/bfce612641a70e106b20b136431f0e4046b5c37f/authz/client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/client/util/HttpMethodAuthenticator.java#L118-L129) the setResponseMode is not taken into account



### Version

18.0

### Expected behavior

AuthorizationRequest  sent to the server has the option to set response_mode

### Actual behavior

AuthorizationRequest  sent to the server does not has the option to set response_mode

### How to Reproduce?

- Send AuthorizationRequest with setResponseMode to decision
 
```
AuthorizationRequest` request = new AuthorizationRequest();
 request.addPermission(resource, scope);
 request.setMetadata(metadata);
```

issue_title: DefaultBruteForceProtector leverages a single thread to write success/failed events
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/managers/DefaultBruteForceProtector.java uses a single thread/queue to update the database for success/failed login attempts.

The idea behind it is to prevent multiple concurrent writes to overwrite each-other. The problem with that though is most installation of Keycloak is leveraging a cluster with multiple nodes, which means this doesn't really do anything other than introduce a bottleneck. 

This is a follow-up to https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/7748, where it was observed that during both success and failed login attempts a wait of up to 5 seconds is introduced waiting for the queue to process the event. It can be questioned if this is a good idea or not for failed login attempts, but it certainly isn't a good idea to do this for successful logins. 

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

_No response_

_No response_

issue_title: post_logout_redirect_uri causes "invalid redirect_uri" for clients created in keycloak 19 with old admin console
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

On our dev system we have clients created in keycloak v17 and clients created in v19.

For example: 
`https://my.keycloak.com/realms/my-realm/protocol/openid-connect/logout?post_logout_redirect_uri=https://valid.redirect.uri&client_id=my-client` should work but did not, even though `https://valid.redirect.uri` is a valid `redirect_uri` configured in `my-client`.

I was able to compare export jsons of old and new clients and found this difference:
```
{
    ...
    "protocol": "openid-connect",
    "attributes": {
        "saml.force.post.binding": "false",
        "saml.multivalued.roles": "false",
        "post.logout.redirect.uris": "+", //<--- this line exists in old but not in the new clients
        "frontchannel.logout.session.required": "true",
        ...
```

This is also the way to hotfix this issue: 
* export the client
* delete exported client from keycloak
* add the line above to the exported json under `"attributes"` object
Line: `"post.logout.redirect.uris": "+"`
* import the edited json 
* redirect works again


### Version

19.0.0

### Expected behavior

That the valid redirect URIs in the client configuration are used to validate post logout redirect URIs.
Or have a separate configuration in each client for valid post-logout URIs.
And - of course - a successful redirect.

### Actual behavior

Error: invalid redirect_uri

### How to Reproduce?

* Create new client in keycloak v19
* log in to client successfully
* build post_logout_redirect_url using URL provided  in `end_session_endpoint` in `realms/my-realm/.well-known/openid-configuration`, adding the following URL params `client_id` & `post_logout_redirect_uri`
* see "Error invalid redirect_uri" both in browser and in logs
`keycloak_1  | 2022-09-06 16:10:00,122 WARN  [org.keycloak.events] (executor-thread-129) type=LOGOUT_ERROR, realmId=my-realm, clientId=null, userId=null, ipAddress=1.1.1.1, error=invalid_redirect_uri, redirect_uri=https://valid.redirect.uri`

issue_title: Exception due to requirement of "client_id" presence within Request Object
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Issue Description:
===================

A thirdparty OIDC client registered in Keycloak is using the authorisation code grant flow.
The authorization request is build by this OIDC client and the `request_uri` request parameter is provided.

When Keycloak tries to handle the JWE Encrypted Request Object obtained through the "request_uri" request parameter, we can see that Keycloak verifies that the "client_id" claim is present within the decrypted request object and is the same as the "client_id" provided request parameter. Request Object provided by a thirdparty OIDC Client doesn't contain this claim as this is not a mandatory claim in the OIDC specifications.

The OIDC specification states that for the request to be a valid OAuth 2.0 Authorization Request, " values for the response_type and client_id parameters MUST be included using the OAuth 2.0 request syntax, since they are REQUIRED by OAuth 2.0. The values for these parameters MUST match those in the Request Object, if present."
[OIDC Specification](
https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#RequestUriParameter
)

The "response_type" and "client_id" request parameters are indeed provided by this thirdparty OIDC client but are not present in the Request Object because these values are optional within it. So the validation against the "client_id" should be done only if the claim is provided in the Request Object only... and then the presence of this claim shouldn't be mandatory as today within Keycloak

**Auth Request Endpoint**
https://www.foo.com/authorize?response_type=code
&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Frfoo.com&request_uri=https%3A%2F%2Ffoo.com/bar&scope=openid%2Bservices&client_id=aaa&state=bbb&nonce=ccc

**Decrypted Request Object**
```
{
"foo": "bar",
"baz": "some"
}
```

server.log
===========

```
org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.request.AuthorizationEndpointRequestParserProcessor.parseRequest(AuthorizationEndpointRequestParserProcessor.java:96)
at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.AuthorizationEndpoint.process(AuthorizationEndpoint.java:132)
at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.AuthorizationEndpoint.buildGet(AuthorizationEndpoint.java:112)
at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)
at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:62)
at sun.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43)
at java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:498)
```

Notes:
=====

The change should be done in the following class
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/endpoints/request/AuthzEndpointRequestObjectParser.java
In particular...

```
if (clientId == null) {
throw new RuntimeException("Request object must be set with the client_id");
}

if (!client.getClientId().equals(clientId.asText())) {
throw new RuntimeException("The client_id in the request object is not the same as the authorizing client");
}
```

should be changed into

```
if (clientId != null && !client.getClientId().equals(clientId.asText())) {
throw new RuntimeException("The client_id in the request object is not the same as the authorizing client");
}
```

Workaround
===========
Updated source file of AuthzEndpointRequestObjectParser
Updated source file of AuthzEndpointRequestParser
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/endpoints/request/AuthzEndpointRequestParser.java
 
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/endpoints/request/AuthzEndpointRequestObjectParser.java

Queries:
=========
1) Is the "client_id" claim mandatory in the object request (current Keycloak mode) ?

2) Is the overriding of each authorization endpoint request attribute, with the one available from the one in the object request even if it is null, correct (current Keycloak mode) ?

3) Regarding the RFC9101 ("The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework: JWT-Secured Authorization Request (JAR)"), the "client_id" claim seems indeed mandatory.
However, it doesn't seem correct to override the authorization endpoint request attributes with null values if not present in the request object.

### Version

19.0.1

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_

Parameters 'client_id' and 'response_type' not strictly required in O… (#14679)

issue_title: Delay LDAPObject creation until mandatory attributes are set
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

If the `syncRegistrations` option is enabled in an LDAP user federation (in order to create users in the LDAP server as soon as they are created in keycloak) current implementation works this way:

1. When the `UserModel` is created at keycloak it is also added in LDAP only with the username and default values provided by the mappers (`UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper`). It is created at [this point](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/18.0.2/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/LDAPStorageProvider.java#L289) calling the method `addUserToLdap`.
2. In the [addUserToLdap](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/18.0.2/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/LDAPUtils.java#L72-L78) the `onRegisterUserToLDAP` method is called for each mapper and the default values are set for the LDAPObject to create.
3. The problem here is that the user object is added to the LDAP server with only the username and the default values. The current code forces [a default empty value](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/18.0.2/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/mappers/UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper.java#L460-L463) for all the mandatory attributes.
4. After the creation the `UserModel` is modified setting the different attributes using typical setters (setFirstName, setEmail, setAttribute,...) and the UserModel and in turn the LDAPObject are modified accordingly.
5. When the transaction is committed the LDAP user is modified accordingly setting the final values.

Let's give an example, having an LDAP configuration with `syncRegistrations` with the following mandatory attributes and no default value (empty value will be used for all of them):

* username - uid
* firstname - cn
* lastname - sn
* email - mail

If the user is created with this `kcadm` command:

```
./kcadm.sh create users -r ldap -s username=ricky -s firstName=ricky -s lastName=ricky -s email=ricky@lala.org
```

First the user is created in LDAP with the following data:

```
DN: uid=ricky,dc=example,dc=com
mail:  
uid: ricky
objectclass: inetOrgPerson
sn:
cn:
```

And at the end of the transaction the entry is modified to the final values provided:

```
DN: uid=ricky,dc=example,dc=com
mail: ricky@lala.org
uid: ricky
sn: ricky
cn: ricky
```

The behavior is weird but the way `UserModel` works forced doing it this way. It's impossible to know when the transaction will finish (and the LDAP object needs to be created before the transaction ends, in order to get the DN and the GUID attribute of the entry created to set them into the `UserModel`). Problems:

1. The RDN attribute can be defined to another attribute different to the username (mail from example) and the entry will be created first with the RDN `mail=defaultvalue` and then renamed to the correct passed mail. This can fail if two users are created at the same time.
2. Any LDAP attribute can be configured as unique and it can fail when using the default attribute (see for example in [i389](https://directory.fedoraproject.org/docs/389ds/howto/howto-uid-uniqueness.html)). It's the same as the RDN, it can fail if two entries are created at the same time (default value will not be unique and one creation would fail).

The solution is delaying the creation of the LDAPObject for the user until all the mandatory attributes are set. As soon as the LDAPObject is complete (in mandatory attrs) the entry will be created. At the end of the transaction the entry will be modified for the rest of attributes (if any other non mandatory attribute is added after all mandatory attributes are set). The modification should ensure backwards compatibility with the current behavior.






### Version

19.0.1

### Expected behavior

See description.

### Actual behavior

See description.

### How to Reproduce?

Configure an LDAP provider with values:

```
syncRegistrations: true
importEnabled: true
usernameLDAPAttribute: uid
usersDn: dc=example,dc=com
userObjectClasses: inetOrgPerson, organizationalPerson
rdnLDAPAttribute: mail
editMode: WRITABLE
```

And add and LDAP mapper for email:

```
providerId: user-attribute-ldap-mapper
name: email
ldap.attribute: mail
user.model.attribute: email
is.mandatory.in.ldap: true
```

When you create the user you will see the steps commented if setting ldap debug (`--log-level=INFO,org.keycloak.storage.ldap:ALL`)



### Anything else?

_No response_

Closes #14286

issue_title: KEYCLOAK_SESSION not working for some user federation setups
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

In some cases with user federation, the ID of the user can contain some special characters. For example when configured LDAP provider with "Import Users" set to OFF and with "UUID LDAP Attribute" set to "uid" . Then when you have some user with special character in the "uid", this is in turn used as the ID of the user. And this is saved in the KEYCLOAK_SESSION cookie:
```
KEYCLOAK_SESSION=**:spécial.char/**; 
KEYCLOAK_SESSION_LEGACY=***; 
```
The character "é" is present in the cookie name, which is not allowed by the undertow server. In the log is this (likely when accessing Keycloak from some Javascript application when the particular user with special character is logged):
```
ERROR [io.undertow.request] (default task-2) UT005023: Exception handling request to /auth/realms/defence-intra/protocol
/openid-connect/login-status-iframe.html: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: UT000173: An invalid control character [233] was 
present in the cookie value or attribute
```

### Version

19.0.1

### Anything else?

For the fix, it is probably easiest to Base64Url encode the cookie `KEYCLOAK_SESSION` (and maybe some other related cookies, not yet sure for which of them is this applicable) to make sure that there are not special characters in these cookies.

issue_title: Keycloak server partial gziped resources in some cases
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

We are serving keycloak theme resources behind CDN.  We have observed partial files been cached occationally.
Looks like the current implementation of https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/encoding/GzipResourceEncodingProvider.java#L42 makes it possible to have a race condition. Request close to the one initially creating the gziped resource can serve partial content. In the general case the subsequent requests will use the complete file. In case of CDN it might take a while for the cached resource to expire

### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should serve correct and complete resource files

### Actual behavior

When concurrent requests for gziped resource are being handled, it is possible to serve partial resource 

### How to Reproduce?

Running multiple requests at single resource requesting gzipped content

### Anything else?

_No response_

```xml
        <dsig:DigestValue>p3ldrwhvbO1zoeHlhCx9TdZ01hGnw3f3IqBDIT5e1oU=</dsig:DigestValue>
      </dsig:Reference>
    </dsig:SignedInfo>
    <dsig:SignatureValue>

      xDNSE6h2cevj4nT7NSQDxoNh1LRBokwjUNJWQg==
```

```xml
ynWLlJTyxUpg+FakcxGNDnSUG6Ofslv6byQDsNY56yvqKCWbcqa1/70PD401E/Gf2XcD4paPAvHX
B+wS25QFytqrxumRtlJiKcPS+IB8umpcHG4mKk3Qg9FxCRQk2Pk693VnEtYyQ5VXUTNFW8SfWpnQ
```

issue_title: [CVE-2022-38750] Stack-based Buffer Overflow vulnerability in org.yaml:snakeyaml
issue_body:
SnakeYAML is a transitive dependency coming from quarkus-kubernetes-client. The mitigation is to upgrade the `quarkus.operator.sdk.version` to 4.0.1 and `quarkus.version` to 2.12.2 inside https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/operator/pom.xml. 

-   Package Manager: maven
-   Vulnerable module: org.yaml:snakeyaml
-   Introduced through: org.keycloak:keycloak-operator@999-SNAPSHOT, io.quarkus:[quarkus-kubernetes-client@2.7.6.Final](mailto:quarkus-kubernetes-client@2.7.6.Final) and others

### Detailed paths

-   _Introduced through_: org.keycloak:keycloak-operator@999-SNAPSHOT › io.quarkus:[quarkus-kubernetes-client@2.7.6.Final](mailto:quarkus-kubernetes-client@2.7.6.Final) › io.smallrye.config:smallrye-config-source-yaml@2.9.2 › org.yaml:snakeyaml@1.30

# Overview

[org.yaml:snakeyaml](https://code.google.com/p/snakeyaml/source/browse/) is a YAML 1.1 parser and emitter for Java.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Stack-based Buffer Overflow when parsing crafted untrusted YAML files, which can lead to a denial-of-service.

# Remediation

Upgrade `org.yaml:snakeyaml` to version 1.31 or higher.

# References

-   [Bitbucket Commit](https://bitbucket.org/snakeyaml/snakeyaml/commits/f3ab4e0f54c37ddb10f00b71d04187bb0ef1799c)
-   [Bitbucket Issue](https://bitbucket.org/snakeyaml/snakeyaml/issues/530/stackoverflow-oss-fuzz-47039)
-   [Chromium Bugs](https://bugs.chromium.org/p/oss-fuzz/issues/detail?id=47039)

issue_title: Default Build Failing Due to Test Failures
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

After cloning and building without making any modifications, I get the following error:

`java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException: NativePRNG SecureRandom not available`

Which appears to occur in

`org.keycloak.crypto.elytron.test.ElytronHmacTest.testHmacSignaturesUsingKeyGen`

### Version

19.0.1

### Expected behavior

Build success.

### Actual behavior

`[ERROR] Errors:`

`[ERROR]   ElytronHmacTest.testHmacSignaturesUsingKeyGen:42 » NoSuchAlgorithm NativePRNG SecureRandom not available`

`[INFO]`

`[ERROR] Tests run: 46, Failures: 0, Errors: 1, Skipped: 2`

`[INFO]`

`[INFO] ------------------------------------------------------------------------`

`[INFO] Reactor Summary for Keycloak 999-SNAPSHOT:`

`[INFO]`

`[INFO] Keycloak BOM Parent ................................ SUCCESS [  1.048 s]`

`[INFO] Keycloak BOM for adapters .......................... SUCCESS [  0.031 s]`

`[INFO] Keycloak BOM for server extensions ................. SUCCESS [  0.028 s]`

`[INFO] Keycloak BOM utilities for the quickstarts ......... SUCCESS [  0.029 s]`

`[INFO] Keycloak ........................................... SUCCESS [  0.054 s]`

`[INFO] Keycloak Common .................................... SUCCESS [ 14.303 s]`

`[INFO] Keycloak Core ...................................... SUCCESS [ 24.565 s]`

`[INFO] Keycloak Server SPI ................................ SUCCESS [  4.574 s]`

`[INFO] Keycloak Server Private SPI ........................ SUCCESS [  7.780 s]`

`[INFO] Keycloak Crypto Parent ............................. SUCCESS [  0.032 s]`

`[INFO] Keycloak Crypto Default ............................ SUCCESS [  7.006 s]`

`[INFO] Keycloak Crypto FIPS 140-2 Integration ............. SUCCESS [  6.586 s]`

`[INFO] Keycloak Crypto Wildfly Elytron Integration ........ FAILURE [  5.754 s]`

### How to Reproduce?

Adding mirror to mvnw's maven-settings.xml [as described in building.md](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/docs/building.md), and running

`mvnw clean install`

Same issue also occurs when not using Maven wrapper.

### Anything else?

Maven Version (mvnw): 3.8.6

Java Version: 11.0.16

Operating System: Windows 10
commit_message:
Distinguish between Windows & other OSes when testing with SecureRandom, to use available PRNG algorithm (#14874)

Closes #14610
